title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first pattern selection and coupling decision record for asynchronous architecture and pattern selection. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of pattern selection and coupling decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Temporal Coupling Decision
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 8721 in a consumer fleet under a shared dependency outage. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Temporal Coupling Decision.

Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Broker Fit Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: RabbitMQ queues are a durable event log and should retain all history indefinitely.
